# Dogfood jam — Ask-mode transcript
<!-- generated by readonly Ask-mode Task subagent [3c8218a3-7ceb-4376-8cdf-64011693ad87] -->

**Branch:** `fix/add-docs-dogfood-readme-md-documenting`  
**Tracking issue:** https://github.com/aditya2kx/jarvis/issues/66  
**Driver:** readonly subagent (Ask mode) — no file edits  

---

## 1. Requirement restatement

We are adding a production-quality `docs/dogfood/README.md` that explains the
purpose of the `docs/dogfood/` directory — it holds end-to-end lifecycle conformance
runs produced by `scripts/dogfood_lifecycle.py`. The file must give any reader
(agent or human) a clear mental model of what is in this directory, how the
dogfood orchestrator works, and how to reproduce a run.

This is distinct from the 8-line `DOGFOOD_DOC` stub inside `dogfood_lifecycle.py`
(lines 573–588) which is written as a throwaway during a mechanical run. The
production README is meaningfully richer and lives permanently in the repo.

---

## 2. Scope clarification

**In scope:**
- `docs/dogfood/README.md` (new file, ~50–80 lines)
- A `PROGRESS.md` dated entry documenting the genuine-flow dogfood run

**Out of scope:**
- Any code changes
- Modifying or replacing the `DOGFOOD_DOC` constant inside `dogfood_lifecycle.py`
- Committing any `lifecycle-run-*.md` transcript files as part of this PR
- Any test file changes

**Assumptions:**
- The README targets both human readers and agent sub-sessions picking up work in this repo
- Style follows the existing `docs/contributing/*.md` pattern: concise, gotcha-focused, not narrative

---

## 3. Key design decisions

**D1 – Step-marker types:** The README must document the four annotation types
found in lifecycle-run transcripts:
- `SEEDED` — substep factually complete the moment `new_requirement` ran
- `HARNESS-DRIVEN` — agent drove this autonomously
- `OPERATOR-SIMULATED` — harness simulated operator approval for gate demo
- `OPERATOR-REAL` — real operator merge (the one requiring your GitHub click)

**D2 – All four subcommands covered:** `run`, `resume`, `check`, `cleanup` —
each with a one-sentence description and the canonical invocation.

**D3 – State file reference:** Note that state is persisted at
`metrics/pr_cost/dogfood-state.json` (gitignored; used by resume/check/cleanup).

**D4 – WORKFLOW.md cross-reference:** The README should point at `docs/WORKFLOW.md §7`
(Verification matrix) where the dogfood entry lives.

**D5 – Regeneration instructions:** A copy-pasteable bash block showing the
full run → pause → resume flow.

---

## 4. Acceptance evidence contract (PR Section 4 template)

```
## 4. End-to-end test (with evidence)

Evidence block 1 — file exists and has expected structure:
```bash
$ ls docs/dogfood/
README.md
$ head -5 docs/dogfood/README.md
# Dogfood evidence
```
Expected: file present, first heading matches.

Evidence block 2 — verify.py --fast passes:
```bash
$ python3 scripts/verify.py --fast
Verify PASSED.
```
Expected: exit 0.

Evidence block 3 — doc-freshness clean:
```bash
$ python3 scripts/check_doc_freshness.py
doc-freshness: N changed file(s); all coupled docs updated. ✓
```
Expected: exit 0 (doc-only change correctly does not trigger coupling rules).

Evidence block 4 — PROGRESS.md entry present:
```bash
$ grep "Dogfood" PROGRESS.md | tail -1
## 2026-06-23 — Dogfood: genuine-flow run ...
```
Expected: a dated entry exists.

Evidence block 5 — no code files changed:
```bash
$ git diff --name-only origin/main
docs/dogfood/README.md
PROGRESS.md
```
Expected: exactly these two files, no .py changes.
```

---

## 5. Summary for issue comment (`--note` text)

Jammed on issue #66. Agreed scope: add one new file (`docs/dogfood/README.md`)
documenting the dogfood evidence directory — its purpose, the four step-marker
types found in transcripts (SEEDED / HARNESS-DRIVEN / OPERATOR-SIMULATED /
OPERATOR-REAL), all four `dogfood_lifecycle.py` subcommands (run/resume/check/cleanup),
the state file path, and a cross-reference to `docs/WORKFLOW.md §7`. Also add a
dated `PROGRESS.md` entry. No code changes, no test changes, no changes to the
`DOGFOOD_DOC` harness stub, no committed transcript files. Evidence contract:
five blocks as specified in PR §4 — file presence, verify.py --fast, doc-freshness,
PROGRESS.md entry, git diff limited to two files.
